skill.name: polish
skill.description: Final quality pass before shipping. Fixes alignment, spacing, consistency, and detail issues that separate good from great.
skill.summary: Run a final product polish pass across UI details, copy, accessibility, responsiveness, and quality.
skill.author: pbakaus

security_audit.summary: The static findings are false positives from Markdown prose and inline code formatting in SKILL.md. No executable scripts, command execution, network access, data exfiltration, prompt injection, or malicious intent were found.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks
low_findings.description: The external command findings at SKILL.md lines 94 and 154 are inline Markdown references to CSS and TypeScript concepts, not Ruby or shell execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both lines use backticks for inline formatting in instructional prose. There is no Ruby code, shell command, interpolation, or execution context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Crypto Substrings
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography findings are caused by ordinary design-related words such as description, design, desktop, and token guidance in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain product polish instructions, not cryptographic APIs or algorithm selection. No hashing, encryption, or password handling is present.

low_findings.title: False Positive: UI Inspection Guidance
low_findings.description: The system reconnaissance findings are product design checks for grid alignment and browser inspector use, not host or environment reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines refer to visual layout checks and browser tooling for UI review. They do not request system enumeration, credential discovery, or sensitive file access.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Responsiveness Heading
low_findings.description: The network reconnaissance finding at SKILL.md line 132 is a section heading about responsive UI behavior, not network scanning.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The heading is followed by mobile, tablet, desktop, touch target, and reflow checks. No network host discovery or connectivity probing is described.

content.value_statement: Small UI inconsistencies can make finished work feel unfinished. This skill guides a final quality pass across visuals, interactions, copy, accessibility, and responsiveness.
actual_capabilities: Reviews alignment, spacing, typography, color, and visual consistency before release.
Checks interactive states including hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, error, and success.
Guides accessibility checks for contrast, focus indicators, labels, keyboard navigation, and alt text.
Reviews responsive behavior across mobile, tablet, and desktop breakpoints.
Prompts final verification through real usage, device testing, and comparison to design intent.
limitations: It is a checklist and review method, not an automated visual testing tool.
It depends on the agent having access to the product, code, or screenshots being reviewed.
It does not replace functional testing or security review.
It references frontend-design guidance that must be available separately.

use_cases.title: Release UI Quality Review
use_cases.description: Run a final pass on a completed feature to catch layout, state, copy, and accessibility issues before shipping.

use_cases.title: Design Implementation QA
use_cases.description: Compare a built interface against design intent and identify spacing, typography, color, and interaction gaps.

use_cases.title: MVP Readiness Triage
use_cases.description: Prioritize polish work when time is limited and focus on issues that most affect usability and trust.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Polish Pass
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a feature is complete and needs a final review.
prompt_templates.prompt: Polish this feature before release. Check visual alignment, spacing, copy consistency, interactive states, and obvious accessibility issues.

prompt_templates.title: Responsive UI Review
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when the interface must work across breakpoints.
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this UI on mobile, tablet, and desktop. Find spacing, reflow, touch target, text sizing, and horizontal scroll issues.

prompt_templates.title: Interaction State Audit
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when buttons, forms, menus, or async flows need refinement.
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit every interactive element. Verify default, hover, focus, active, disabled, loading, error, and success states are consistent and accessible.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Release Triage
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when shipping soon and polish work must be prioritized.
prompt_templates.prompt: Perform a release polish triage. Separate must-fix issues from nice-to-have refinements, and explain the user impact of each recommendation.

output_examples.input: Review a checkout confirmation page before launch.
output: Primary action alignment is inconsistent with the summary column.
Success copy uses two capitalization styles.
Focus state is missing on the receipt download button.
Mobile spacing collapses too tightly below the order total.

output_examples.input: Polish a dashboard card grid for desktop and mobile.
output: Card gutters should use the same spacing token at every breakpoint.
Long metric labels need wrapping rules to avoid overflow.
Loading placeholders should reserve the final content height.
Empty states need a clear recovery action.

output_examples.input: Triage final UI improvements for a release today.
output: Fix keyboard focus visibility before release.
Fix mobile horizontal scroll before release.
Defer icon style cleanup unless it affects comprehension.

best_practices: Use this skill only after the feature is functionally complete.
Check the interface at real supported viewport sizes and interaction states.
Prioritize polish issues by user impact and release timing.
anti_patterns: Do not spend polish time on unfinished flows that still need core functionality.
Do not perfect one component while leaving common system issues unresolved.
Do not introduce visual refinements without retesting affected behavior.

faq.question: What does this skill help with?
faq.answer: It guides a final quality pass for UI details, interaction states, copy, accessibility, responsiveness, and release readiness.

faq.question: Does it automatically test my application?
faq.answer: No. It provides a structured review process. The agent still needs access to the app, code, or visual evidence.

faq.question: When should I use it?
faq.answer: Use it after the main feature works and before release, handoff, or final stakeholder review.

faq.question: Can it help with accessibility?
faq.answer: Yes. It prompts checks for contrast, focus indicators, labels, keyboard navigation, alt text, and readable mobile text.

faq.question: Is it only for designers?
faq.answer: No. It is useful for frontend engineers, designers, product managers, and QA reviewers.

faq.question: Does it include security analysis?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on product polish and frontend quality. Security review should be handled separately.
